response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with a strong testing culture and some high-throughput systems exposure, but he misses the role's hard minimums by a wide margin. Professional C++ amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, against a requirement of 8 years; his systems-level work is mostly in DLang and Node.js. Large-scale infrastructure experience is limited to about two years on a high-volume microservices team, and he has never held a formal technical-leadership role setting strategy across teams. This profile fits a senior full-stack or product engineering role far better than a C++ cloud-compute platform lead.
strengths: Nearly 20 years of broad engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and systems work
Low-latency, high-throughput microservices experience handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing: authored test frameworks, ran multi-day courses, workshops, and code katas
concerns: Roughly 1 year of professional C++ versus the required 8 years; C++ is not even listed in his skills section
Thin large-scale infrastructure and cloud compute platform experience; most work is product-focused full-stack development
No formal technical-lead experience owning cross-team technical strategy, priorities, and deliverables

key_factors.factor: Limited large-scale infrastructure and cloud platform ownership
